heading: Memorandum of understanding for provision of services of the United States Capitol telephone exchange for the House
label: 2168 Memorandum of understanding for provision of services of the United States Capitol telephone exchange for the House
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (d) of amended .section 215 of div. G of Pub. L. 108–447section 215 of div. G of Pub. L. 108–447section 293 of this titleSection is from the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005, which is div. G of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2005.
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (d) of amended .section 215 of div. G of Pub. L. 108–447section 215 of div. G of Pub. L. 108–447section 293 of this titleSection is from the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005, which is div. G of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2005.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives and the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate may enter into a memorandum of understanding under which the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper shall provide all services of the United States Capitol telephone exchange for the House of Representatives, in accordance with such terms and conditions as may be provided in the memorandum of understanding.

children.heading: Transfer of positions and personnel
children.label: b Transfer of positions and personnel

children.text: all positions in the United States Capitol telephone exchange for which the employing authority is the Chief Administrative Officer shall be transferred to the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper;

all employees in the United States Capitol telephone exchange for whom the employing authority is the Chief Administrative Officer shall be transferred to, and appointed by, the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper; and
the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper shall serve as the employing authority for all personnel of the United States Capitol telephone exchange.

children.label: A
children.text: the employee does not remain in the same position with the exchange; or

children.label: B
children.text: the rate of pay or leave accrual is reduced for cause; and

any leave accrued by the employee that remains unused as of such date shall be transferred to the employee and made available for the employee to use under the same terms and conditions that applied to the use of the leave prior to such date.

children.heading: Reimbursement of expenses by House
children.label: e Reimbursement of expenses by House

children.text: A memorandum of understanding under this section may include a provision requiring the reimbursement by the House of Representatives during a fiscal year (paid out of the applicable accounts of the House) of the expenses incurred by the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper during the fiscal year in carrying out the memorandum with respect to the employees of the United States Capitol telephone exchange whose employing authority prior to the effective date of the memorandum was the Chief Administrative Officer.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a reimbursement for salaries or agency contributions and related expenses, shall be deposited in the account under the heading “” or “”, under the heading “”; andOffice of the sergeant at arms and doorkeeperagency contributions and related expensesSalaries, Officers and Employees

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a reimbursement for expenses, shall be deposited in the account under the heading “” under the heading “”.sergeant at arms and doorkeeper of the senateContingent Expenses of the Senate
Any funds deposited under paragraph (2) shall be available in like manner and for the same purposes as are other funds in the account to which the funds were deposited.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: f Effective date
children.text: This section and the amendment made by this section shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2005 and each succeeding fiscal year.
